rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LAUB REVOCABLE TR. DTD. 1/21/04 AMENDED 8/28/13 TRUST
rulings.Rulings: Petition for Instructions is denied. The Trust has not been named as a defendant in the civil action. The Petition offers no supporting authority for the Court to issue protective orders over prior distributions out of the Trust against a potential creditor of decedent. It is unclear from the record whether Steven Laub was properly appointed Successor Trustee under the Trust and/or the August 2015 settlement agreement. If Mr. Laub was not properly appointed Successor Trustee, he does not have standing to bring this Petition on behalf of the Trust. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, August 23, 2017.

Petition for Instructions is denied. As to the argument that Plaintiff's claim is time barred, Judge Kahn already determined in a November 18, 2016 order overruling the personal representative's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint that the Plaintiff's civil claims were not time-barred. The Petition offers no supporting authority for a preemptive bar to be placed on Plaintiff's civil claims. The Petition does not show that the personal representative has exercised ordinary care and diligence in protecting and preserving the estate by, for example, determining whether decedent's law firm held relevant insurance at the time of the alleged harassment, or attempting to settle the insolvent estate out of the case. The Petition makes no showing that it is in the estate's interest for the personal representative to no longer participate in the civil litigation. The Petition does not address the risk of, for example, default judgment or discovery sanctions being entered against the estate if he is excused from representing the estate against Plaintiff's civil action, while the estate is still a named defendant. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Wednesday, August 23, 2017.
